content: Read ceo/4cf2171d-4600-46a8-84bf-93939bc26aaa/proposal-1-clinerules.md and disposition.json in full; confirm it was never approved via /ceo:approve-initiative before treating it as superseded.
Write a supersession note as a new file inside ceo/4cf2171d-.../ (SUPERSEDED-BY.md) rather than editing the sealed trail's existing files -- sealed trails are immutable per trail-schema.md.

Draft root-level .clinerules as the Colony Constitution: restates EC-002 (Single Dispatcher), MAAI-001/ARCH-TERM-HIL-001 (HIL approval for TYPE1), and the TYPE1/TYPE2 boundary from hil-gating.md, plus the new rule -- no TYPE1 or TYPE2 tool action without an active trail UUID open under .pmcro/trails/. Explicitly note in the file header that this repurposes the .clinerules filename away from the not-recommended Cline Memory Bank proposal in ceo/4cf2171d, per explicit CEO direction on 2026-07-19.

Edit catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/skills/orchestrator/skills/orchestrator-role/SKILL.md step 3 (Plan -> Make -> Check -> Reflect) to add a Linguistic Hand-off requirement: each role's *.jsonl entry must open by addressing the role that handed off to it (e.g. maker's first line addresses planner), and checker step 4 must fail the cycle if hand-off addressing is absent -- checker's own trail schema disposition line gets a new 'handoff_verified' boolean.

Edit catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/skills/orchestrator/commands/brief-session.md Round Table section to read each cycle's plan/make/check/reflect hand-off addressing lines and play them back verbatim (persona-voiced) rather than only reading frame.json + disposition.json as it does today.

Flag for checker: this cycle writes to catalog/ (orchestrator-role/SKILL.md, brief-session.md) -- per hil-gating.md and orchestrator-role's own step 4, this forces disposition to needs-approval regardless of check outcome. Do not attempt to self-seal accept.

This plan's own first line is not addressed to anyone since planner is the first role in the chain with no predecessor to hand off from within this cycle -- orchestrator's own dispatch is the hand-off planner receives, noted here to satisfy the new rule being introduced retroactively to this cycle's own plan.jsonl.
